title: alnus viridis f. subvestita
intro: Alnus viridis f. subvestita, also known as the green alder or green alder-leaved alder, is a variant of the green alder plant (Alnus viridis) that has a distinctive subvestita foliage. The leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are said to have various health benefits. Alnus viridis f. subvestita has been used to treat wounds, reduce swelling, and fight off infections due to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The plant is often prepared as an herbal tea or infused in hot water to harness its medicinal properties, and it can also be dried and used in herbal remedies.
